trade_name: NSQ/ TILING AND STONEWORK LEVEL II 2: TILING AND DECOREATIVE STONEWORK

learning_outcomes.description: Recognize health, safety and welfare measures on construction sites

performance_criteria.description: Explain health, safety and environment relating to tiling and stone decorative work
Comply with health and safety regulations relating to tiling and stone decorative work
Carry out activities in a safe and responsible manner in accordance with legislative, official guidance and manufacturer’s instruction.
Communicate Recognize health and safety issues relevant to tiling and stone decorative work
Demonstrate how to report accidents and emergencies to appropriate personnel such as; • First Aiders • Supervisors/Managers • Health and Safety workers

learning_outcomes.description: Demonstrate the use of personal protective gears on construction sites

performance_criteria.description: Use clean and smart and appropriate PPE
Report accidents and emergencies to appropriate personnel such as; • First Aiders • Supervisors/Managers • Health and Safety workers
Clean and store PPE appropriately after use

learning_outcomes.description: Demonstrate safety considerations on construction site

performance_criteria.description: Identify the location of the first aid box on site.
Describe and give examples of key accidents, trends, causes and prevention on site.
Explain health and safety considerations involved in tiling and stone decorative projects work.

units.title: Communication Systems in a Work Environment

learning_outcomes.description: Use non-complex communication systems in a work environment

performance_criteria.description: Use simple verbal means to pass on necessary information.
Use nonverbal means to pass on necessary information e.g. Body Language.
Interpret symbols and signs in accordance with instructions.

learning_outcomes.description: Identify sources of information in a work environment

performance_criteria.description: Locate the sources of information in an organization and work environment.
Recognize the sources of information in a workplace.
Use various information flow systems in a work environment.
Use information to avoid challenges in a work situation.
Report Implement findings in accordance with procedure in a work environment.

learning_outcomes.description: Demonstrate the use of various communication means in a work environment

performance_criteria.description: Locate Recognize the various communication equipment in the work environment
Use effectively the various communication equipment in a work environment in accordance specifications. .
Pass information effectively to the right personnel in accordance with instructions.
Pass information using symbols, signs and codes.
Select instructions in line with ethics of the work environment.

learning_outcomes.description: Develop a Positive working relationship with colleagues

performance_criteria.description: Recognize the need for positive working relationships with colleagues.
Explain the importance of relating with other people in a way that makes them feel valued and respected.
Use team members when required.
Recognize the personnel when request for assistance.
Explain information to colleagues about own work that might affect others.

learning_outcomes.description: Take responsibility within the team

performance_criteria.description: Recognize own role and responsibility within the team.
Perform individual tasks in line with the team rules and regulations.
Carry out in teamwork in accordance with instructions.

learning_outcomes.description: Comply with policy of organization

performance_criteria.description: Work Implement in line with organizational policy
Use organization code of practice.
Explain the organizational code of conduct.

learning_outcomes.description: Interpret Information on surfaces to be tiled/laid with stone.

performance_criteria.description: Explain the use of specifications on tile/stonework
Interpret tiling/stonework working drawings
Measure the area (Square Meters) of surfaces to be tiled/laid with stonework from the working drawings

learning_outcomes.description: Carryout tiling and stone decorative work using hand tools, power tools and scaffolds

performance_criteria.description: Select tools for tiling and stone decorative work such as: - • Trowel • Spirit Level • Straight Edge • Line • Portable electric saw machine and drills • Standard Manual Tile Cutter • Light tile cutter (glass or diamond cutter) • Tile Spacers • Measuring tape • Pinches • Rubber/Wooden Mallet • Laser leveling and measuring tools • Stone clamps • Stone trimming hammer • Scrapper • Compacting rammers • Concrete/mortar mixers.
Use the required hand tools suitable for tiling and stone decorative work.
Use the required power tools suitable for tiling and stone decorative work.

learning_outcomes.description: Set out areas to be tiled or stone laid

performance_criteria.description: Use measuring the required tools to measure and take levels for tiling/stonework
Use the required tools to set out measured surfaces to be tiled/laid with stone
Prepare Mortar with smooth sand & Lime or cement
Describe tiles/stonework on surfaces in line with specifications.
Carry out gauge & alignment in accordance with specifications
Carry out housekeeping on site in accordance with instructions.

learning_outcomes.description: Erect scaffold, working platforms and ladders at work place.

performance_criteria.description: Identify the types and quantity of materials required for erecting a particular scaffold, working platforms and ladders at the workplace
Use the required tools and equipment for erecting various scaffold/working platform/ladder in accordance with specifications.
Use scaffolding materials to and from site in accordance with instructions.
Recognize the hazard and risk in erecting of scaffold, platform and ladders.
Assemblebase for scaffold and ladders and erect scaffold in accordance with instructions

learning_outcomes.description: Dismantle scaffolds, working platforms and ladders at the workplace

performance_criteria.description: Select the required tools and equipment to dismantle various scaffold/working platform/ladder at the workplace.
Identify hazard and risk in dismantling of scaffold platform and ladders
Dismantle scaffold, platforms and ladders, in accordance with specified guidelines.

learning_outcomes.description: Store appropriately dismantled scaffold.

performance_criteria.description: Maintain dismantled scaffold materials in accordance with instructions.
Dismantled scaffold component in accordance with instructions.
Dispose defective scaffold materials in accordance with instructions.

units.title: Preparation for Tiling and Stonework on Wall/Floor surfaces

learning_outcomes.description: Prepare wall surface for tiling and stone laying

performance_criteria.description: Prepare surfaces for wall /floor tiling and stone laying in accordance with specifications
Select tools and equipment for surface preparation.
Prepare surfaces before placing decorative stonework/tiles

learning_outcomes.description: Select equipment for tiling and stonework for large/wide areas.

performance_criteria.description: Select equipment and tools required for tiling and stonework including: i. Leveling Instrument ii. Compacting rammers (Manual/ Mechanical) iii. Concrete / Mortar mixer iv. Mechanical tile Cutter v. Portable electric saw etc
Use tools and equipment for tiling and decorative stonework
Maintain equipment at the end of each workday.

learning_outcomes.description: Cut tiles/stone according to specification

performance_criteria.description: Carry out tiles/stone to flush with openings
Describe fractional tiles/stones as measured to fit to corners and edges
Cut tiles/stones to size around electrical/plumbing fittings
Construct tiles/stonework for dedo and skirting

learning_outcomes.description: Interprets working drawings to provide the materials and cost estimates.

performance_criteria.description: Measure the tile area (square meter) from the working drawings
Describe the number of tiles required to cover one square meter
State the number of tiles per carton of different sizes

learning_outcomes.description: Determine the quantity of tile required by calculations for the areas to be covered

performance_criteria.description: Measure surface areas of different shapes and sizes to be tiled
Use the number of tiles required to cover one meter square
Use the quantity of tile sizes that will cover a given surface area
State the number of tile cartons required for given surface area.

learning_outcomes.description: Measure and set out wall/floor surfaces

performance_criteria.description: Read and interpret working drawings of a simple building project.
Use instruments to measure various linear dimensions
Measure internal/external surfaces
Select an area for tiling from an internal wall/floor surface e.g kitchen.
Measure the length and height of the wall to be tiled

learning_outcomes.description: Fix Tiles on wall/floor surfaces

performance_criteria.description: Determine the line of symmetry, start tiling from there and move both ways
Mix adhesive materials in accordance with specifications.
Select the tiles for the work.
Soak the tiles in water for the required duration of time
Remove the tiles from water and allow it to drain
Apply mortar evenly on the unglazed side of the tiles
Set the tiles in position and tap gently with mallet
Use spirit level to check vertical and horizontal plumb respectively
Reconcile tile level gauge in line with the specifications

learning_outcomes.description: Fill tiling joints with suitable adhesives

performance_criteria.description: Mix the required ratio of adhesives e.g. white cement, OPC etc
Apply adhesives into the joints to flush with tile surface laid
Clean the tile surface with suitable cleaner

learning_outcomes.description: Tile laying of internal Corners

performance_criteria.description: Set tiles from the middle towards the corner; and check if the last one at the corner is fully tiled
Apply adhesive on the last corner tile and set it to position.
Use square to confirm the angle if it is 90" and check if the corner tile is fractional
Measure the size on the tile mark and cut the tile according to the size
Apply adhesive on the tile and fix it to the surface

learning_outcomes.description: Tile laying of external corners

performance_criteria.description: Set tiles from the middle towards the corner; and check if the last one at the corner is fully tiled
Apply adhesive on the last corner tile and set it to position.
Use square to confirm the angle if it is 90" and check if the corner tile is fractional
Measure the size on the tile mark and cut the tile according to the size
Apply adhesive on the tile and fix it to the surface

learning_outcomes.description: Set and lay Glass Blocks

performance_criteria.description: Identify the areas to lay glass block
Measure the area to lay glass blocks on site
Calculate the area of glass block required in square meters (m2)
Set and level first course of blocks
Use adhesive to bind set blocks
Lay glass blocks and ensure alignment (vertical and horizontal)
Clean the surface using water or other recommended chemicals

learning_outcomes.description: Set and lay mosaic tiles

performance_criteria.description: Select tools such as: • Utility knife • Measuring tape • Notched trowel • Tile saw • Tile Nippers • Margin trowel • Drill • Pencil • Floating board etc.
Select appropriate bonding materials/adhesives
Dry lift the tiles and center the design
Fix mosaic to required position using bonding agents

units.title: Set Out and Lay Stone Floor Surface

learning_outcomes.description: Set out surfaces to receive stones

performance_criteria.description: Identify the areas to be laid with stone from the working drawing
Measure the area to be laid with stone
Set out the surface using tools in accordance with instructions.

learning_outcomes.description: Calculate the quantity of stones required

performance_criteria.description: Measure the dimensions of surface to be laid with stone
Calculate the surface areas to be stoned in square meter (m2)
Determine the quantity of stones required for the calculated area

learning_outcomes.description: Cut stones according to site requirements

performance_criteria.description: Select manual cutting tools e.g Diamond tile cutter/stone chipping hammer
Place the straight edge or square on the tile/stone along the marked line and use the appropriate tool to cut
Cut fractional tiles/stone of different sizes e.g. 50mm, 75mm, 80mm etc

learning_outcomes.description: Select adhesives to be used according to specifications and conditions

performance_criteria.description: Select adhesive for stone work
Use adhesive for stones work
Use cement mixes according to the conditions e.g Damp areas will require harder mixes; dry areas will require lighter mixes

learning_outcomes.description: Lay stones to specification

performance_criteria.description: Prepare a screed bed to receive stones
Lay stone from center to run either direction and ensure alignment
Use the straight edge, line and level to check alignment
Fill the joints with appropriate cement and clean the surface

units.title: Forming Sand & Cement, Cement & Chippings or Granite Mix for

learning_outcomes.description: Select tools/equipment and materials for terrazzo work

performance_criteria.description: Select hand tools and power tools suitable for terrazzo work such as: • Trowel • Spirit Level • Straight Edge • Line • roller and compactor • Concrete Mixer • Metal Square • Hack saw • Washing machine
Select appropriate materials suitable for terrazzo
Use tools and equipment suitable for terrazzo work

learning_outcomes.description: Place floor screeds to level

performance_criteria.description: Prepare the floor surface, to receive ebonite strip
Divide the area into panels or Bays using ebonite strips suitable for terrazzo thickness
Measure sand and cement according to specified ratio
Mix sand and cement with water to requirement
Mix and spread the screed into each panel, level and compact to float

learning_outcomes.description: Cast and Polish Terrazzo

performance_criteria.description: Bed ebonite strips according to the design panels with mortar
Cure the mortar for at least 24 hours
Measure cement and granite according to the specified ratio
Mix and spread the granite paste into each bay
Level and compact to float with roller
Cure the surface after it has hardened in at least 24 hours
Continue curing up to maximum of 28 days
Grind the surface
Polish the surface
